message:
Zero-allocation Connection header parsing for common tokens

Optimize ConnectionInfo::parse_connection_header() to avoid allocations
in the hot path for common Connection header values.

Before:
- Every token was lowercased via to_ascii_lowercase() (allocates)
- Match statement compared against lowercase strings
- Even "close", "keep-alive", "upgrade" caused allocations

After:
- Use eq_ignore_ascii_case() for known tokens (no allocation)
- Only allocate for custom hop-by-hop headers (rare path)
- Most requests use standard tokens and never allocate

Also optimize is_standard_hop_by_hop_header():
- Use iter().any() with eq_ignore_ascii_case() instead of creating
  a lowercase copy and checking set membership
- Eliminates allocation for every header check

Performance impact:
- Common case (close/keep-alive): Zero allocations vs 1 allocation per token
- Rare case (custom header): Same allocation cost as before
- is_standard_hop_by_hop_header: Zero allocations vs 1 allocation per call

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
